title: AI agents are the future of work
author: Zain Kahn November

- AI agents are the future of work
AI agents are the future of work
ALSO: WeWork bankruptcy, blocking blockers, and peak AI hype
Read time: 2.5 minutes
Welcome back, Superhuman
Claims of Apple’s demise in the AI race may be greatly exaggerated, as the company announces new powerhouse chips that pack a serious AI punch.
And AI agents are the next big thing in AI, with rumors swirling that OpenAI may announce an AI agent next week. But what are they? And how will they impact our jobs?
TODAY’S MENU
Apple joins the AI party with new M3 chips
AI agents could be the future of work
5 AI tools to supercharge your productivity
Key developments in AI governance and policy

TODAY IN AI & TECH
WeWork Inc stock price. Source: Google Finance
We(no longer)Work: WeWork is reportedly on the verge of announcing bankruptcy.
Source of Truth: Data that label images as AI-generated are coming to phones and cameras.
Blocking Blockers: YouTube announces it’s cracking down on ad blockers and will stop users with ad blockers from viewing videos.
Teacher’s Pet: A new project from Microsoft uses generative AI to help teachers quickly develop personalized lesson plans.
Peak Hype? Collins Dictionary names AI as word of the year.
NEXT IN AI
Apple joins the AI party with new M3 chips and Macs
Apple
While Microsoft and Google have been showcasing their AI products for months, Apple has been unusually quiet. And finally we may know why, as Apple shows off its new M3 chips which come with serious AI chops.
Apple’s new MacBook Pros might have stolen the show at their Scary Fast event this week, but it’s what’s under the hood of these computers that’s got everyone in AI to sit up and take note.
Key insights directly from the press release:
Support for up to 128GB of memory on these chips unlocks workflows previously not possible on a laptop, such as AI developers working with even larger transformer models with billions of parameters.
The Neural Engine is up to 60% faster than in the M1 family of chips, making AI/ML workflows even faster while keeping data on device to preserve privacy. Super helpful for image and video work.
On the M3 Max, the 40-core GPU is up to 50 percent faster than M1 Max.
The announcement comes at a key time for Apple, as competitors race to build faster chips. Chipmaker Qualcomm recently announced the Snapdragon X, a PC chip that it claims is better than Apple’s M2 processor.
If you want a more detailed breakdown of what Apple’s new chips can do, check out this blog.

AI AT WORK
AI agents are about to go mainstream. How will they affect workers and their jobs?
The best way to understand what’s next in technology is to see what the scientists and hackers are tinkering with. And lately, AI agents — programs that can make decisions or perform a service based on their environment — look set to take the stage as the next major AI innovation.
AI agents go beyond what chatbots like ChatGPT can do by talking and collaborating with other apps and websites to accomplish a task. Over the last year, researchers have been able to get software agents to do a host of different tasks like:
Play video games
Create a virtual interactive town where agents simulate human behavior
Simulate the roles of software engineers in a company
While the tech hasn’t yet seen a working breakthrough comparable to ChatGPT, it’s hypothetically capable of both replacing and assisting in tasks that currently require human effort, like:
Edit calendars and schedule appointments
Order food and supplies online
Enter and analyze data, and share insights through emails and other communication channels autonomously
While no one can predict the future with certainty, here are a few potential outcomes in the event that AI agents go mainstream:
Assistance: AI agents are sophisticated enough to automate low-level tasks like generating and sharing action items from a meeting, but not advanced enough to execute action items resulting from these meetings.
Replacement: These agents are so advanced that they can outright replace large parts of human jobs. In this scenario, people are either freed up do more important work at their jobs, or they get replaced outright and have to find other jobs.
New jobs: Automation creates new types of jobs that only humans can do. Alternatively, the bar for entrepreneurship may be lowered as people hire AI agents and use them as cheap labor to build products and companies.
Nothing: AI agents could, of course, turn out to be all hype and no substance.
GOVERNANCE AND POLICY
The most notable developments in AI governance and policy
Biden releases AI executive order directing agencies to develop safety guidelines
US President Biden issued an executive order on generative AI, establishing interim guidelines until official legislation is enacted. It directs government authorities to create AI safety standards, combat cybersecurity dangers, and avoid prejudice. Read more →
Governments must not rush into policing AI
The Economist argues that governments should regulate AI with caution to speed up technological innovation, ahead of key AI summit in the UK. Read more →
United Nations creates advisory body to address AI governance
The United Nations has established a 39-member advisory committee to supervise worldwide AI governance. The body is made up of industry leaders, government officials and academics. Read more →
AI TUTORIAL
How to decode a prompt for any image using ChatGPT
Here’s your guide:
First, go to GPT and upload an image.
Enter the prompt: “I want to generate an image similar to this one. Can you please give me a prompt for DALL-E 3 to create similar image like this one.”
Now, if you want to generate similar images, copy the prompt results.
Switch to DALLE-3, paste the prompt, and hit enter.
